I wrote the review of more than 3 paragraphs from my own experiences but 
even the review got wiped out just like the problem report page of this app 
had wiped out my entire text and location on the map, and an attached 
picture. It took more than 15 trials, and after 3 hours later, and without 
being able to save the texts in another app as this SeeClickFix app will 
wipe out any filled in information all wiped again. Thisnwas way too 
frustrating. Anonymity setting is a joke and by the time I disputed from 
the falsely closed report and changed the report status as open again, the 
app disclosed my identity altogether. And the developer wrongly afvised me 
that the profile name can be changed but it won't allow any change. This 
"falsely" closed report status (and archiving by the facilitator) is 
alarming to me - our town employees are figuring out to dismiss the report 
by simply changing the report status to closed at any time. That is 
unethical. The case should be only closed when both parties changed to 
"closed" -only thdn, the facilitator can archive it (unpin) from the map. 
And once archived, the public can never find what was discussed and 
concluded in the past similar repirt and resolution. Nothing will be 
learned as the past report will be hidden from the public access later. The 
app doesn't adopt the "review" system to prevent or discourage the false 
closing by the recipient of the report without any explanation or 
resolution provided. The closing should be controlled by the reporting 
party and not by the town departments. As Insaid my biggest problem is, The 
town department and its facilitator (IT staff) had falsely CLOSED and 
ARCHIVED the reports, and this app never notified the reporter of its newly 
changed status as an alert from the app to the reporter's phone. Lots of 
loop holes to allow the town to dismiss the reports blatantly. Implementing 
reviews by the original reporter can discourage this foul acts.

This app used to be great. I've reported close to 800 instances of graffiti in the last 7 months in my neighborhood. But over the last couple of months the app (and website) has become impossibly slow and an increasing number of problems makes it a frustrating experience to use. I'm better off calling 311 at this point. Some current problems: it's SLOW, and frequently drops reports. I might have to submit and resubmit a report 3 or 4 times to get it through. It's the same when updating the location. Super slow. I can stand there waiting an additional half minute waiting for a response from the server. Oh and when I get to write in the report the app has a black background with black text. That's right. You can't even see what you're typing. Every update introduces a new problem. When I log in to the website, it asks me to confirm if a report I made is mine or not. I made the report while signed in to my SeeClickFix account on my phone. Why do I need to confirm it? When I do I it takes the webpage a good 10-30 seconds to refresh. Just additional frustration and more wasted time. I've called, emailed, and attempted to use their support forum (which also doesn't work) to no avail.

I love this app! Works well and is very convenient. I found the tips on zooming into the map to a specific address and to be as descriptive as possible very helpful. I reported several potholes with some pictures with mostly successful responses. The city fixed the issues within the week, and others were acknowledged, but has yet-to-be-fixed/ignored. Although I always fill in the description blanks, the app does not necessarily submit the information. And you do not notice this until sometime after you have submitted your issue. For photo submissions, I did find it easier to take your photos prior to using the app (which you then search and select from your photo gallery). Once you have submitted your issue, you are emailed an notification of your issue, which you can check on its status. One star off for some description and picture improvements to be made. Note: GTA-Toronto location.
